//: Tepna — Phase 4a machine-checked contracts (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-AND-FRONTIER brief). CI-ONLY: tsc --noEmit --checkJs. NO emit, NO shipped build, NO runtime dep — the 100%-local/offline invariant (CLAUDE.md §5.3) is untouched; TypeScript is a dev-time gate only. Scope is forward-first: only the clean CORE adapter layer is checked, NOT the legacy node *-dsp.js (those carry their own known DOM/localStorage debts, gated separately by the DSP-purity assertion, Phase 4b).
//d2: OWN-THE-BUILD-FOLLOWUPS §5 D.2 — widen this one module at a time, and NEVER blind: prove the module green with `npx -y -p typescript@5.5.4 tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` (the exact pin types.yml runs) BEFORE adding it. Measured 2026-07-13 against this baseline: kernel-constants 0 (ADDED — needed no source edit) · clock 1 · metric-registry 2 · event-coupling 7 · dex-forget 8 · pulsedex-dsp 16 · glucodex-dsp 22 · cpapdex-dsp 27 · integrator-dsp 30 · ecgdex-dsp 31 · ppgdex-dsp 31 · hrvdex-dsp 46 · oxydex-dsp 107. NOTE clock.js and metric-registry.js are SHARED SPINE — inlined into every bundle — so editing either to satisfy tsc (clock's lone error is the `module.exports = root.DexClock` alias at :136) moves all 10 manifestHashes and churns every fixture. That is a deliberate fleet-churn decision, not a free widening. ADDED 2026-07-13 (all NON-bundled → zero manifestHash churn): event-coupling.js · dex-coload.js · provenance-banner.js — each `root.X = …` export cast via `/** @type {any} */ (root).X = …` (the IIFE-arg type wins over a @param, and `module.exports = X` poisons the `this` fallback), and event-coupling's Node-CLI `process` reached through `/** @type {any} */(globalThis).process`. nsrr-adapter.js (2 errs) DEFERRED — it references a bare `processNight` global (a runtime dependency oxydex-dsp doesn't export), not a type-only fix. ADDED 2026-07-13 (2nd pass): pulsedex-dsp.js — its 16 errors were ALL TS2304 for co-loaded globals (DexClock·DexExport·SignalFrame·dexScrubExport), so declaring them in `dex-globals.d.ts` (ambient, `any`) cleared it with ZERO source edit → NO re-bundle. KEY: a bundled *-dsp.js whose only errors are co-loaded-global TS2304 costs NOTHING (add the name to dex-globals.d.ts, no churn). Only real TS2339/property or DSP-reaches-render-sibling errors force a source edit + re-bundle. Remaining DSPs still carry those (glucodex 13·ecgdex 16·cpapdex 16·ppgdex 22·hrvdex 30·integrator 24·oxydex 90) — mixed TS2339 + render-sibling reach-ins (setStatus/UP/safeEl…), each a real fix, not a free add. ADDED 2026-07-13 (3rd pass): signal-orchestrate.js — 0 errors, zero source edit → NO re-bundle (it is inlined into Data Unifier.html / OverDex.html, but a tsconfig-include add never touches a bundle; same free class as kernel-constants.js). It is the shared UI-free node-orchestration middle (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-FOLLOWUPS §1), so gating it covers code two orchestrators depend on. FREE-DSP PATH NOW EXHAUSTED: re-measured every remaining *-dsp.js against this baseline — NONE is free. glucodex/ecgdex/cpapdex/ppgdex are pure TS2339/arithmetic (real fixes); integrator+oxydex mix TS2339+possibly-null; hrvdex is the closest (28 undefined-name + only 2 real TS2339 `.checked` on HTMLElement) BUT its undefined names are render-sibling app-layer helpers (setStatus/setProgress/rerender/getProfile/inferFromData/calcVo2Cat) — declaring THOSE as ambient `any` would mask the very DSP-reaches-render-sibling smell dex-globals.d.ts must NOT hide (it is for SHARED co-loaded spine only, not per-node render siblings), so hrvdex needs a genuine source edit (cast the two `.checked` reads to HTMLInputElement) + a re-bundle, not a free declaration. So the next DSP is a real behavioral-gate change, not a zero-churn add. ADDED 2026-07-13 (4th pass): hrvdex-dsp.js — the FIRST non-free (behavioral-gate) DSP. Three moves: (1) the 2 real TS2339 `.checked`-on-HTMLElement reads cast `/** @type {HTMLInputElement} */(el).checked` (comment-only → EXPORT-INERT: executed JS unchanged, fixture OUTPUT byte-identical); (2) genuine SHARED spine DexKernel (kernel-constants.js) + DexUnits (quantity.js) added to dex-globals.d.ts (same class as DexClock); (3) the per-node UI-sibling reach-ins (setStatus/setProgress/rerender/inferFromData/getProfile/calcVo2Cat — defined in hrvdex-app/render/profile.js) declared in a NEW, SEPARATE `hrvdex-globals.d.ts`, NOT dex-globals.d.ts — keeping the shared file spine-only (per the rule above) while documenting the DSP→UI coupling as node-specific debt (delete that file once the hrvdex UI files join the gate). Because a `.checked` cast is comment-only, the re-bundle is export-inert: HRVDex manifestHash moved 229842e56f9f→dafad1d5bb27 (and Data Unifier/OverDex, which co-load hrvdex-dsp.js, were re-bundled too) but every fixture OUTPUT hash is unchanged (GATE A + GATE B green; the real-recording hrvdex equiv leg is uploads-gated so it is SKIP here and in CI, same as every real-recording node). PATTERN for the remaining non-free DSPs (glucodex/ecgdex/cpapdex/ppgdex/integrator/oxydex): shared spine → dex-globals.d.ts, node UI siblings → a per-node <node>-globals.d.ts, real DOM/property errors → cast in source + re-bundle (watch the two orchestrators when the DSP is co-loaded). ADDED 2026-07-13 (5th pass): glucodex-dsp.js — 13 errors, ALL self-contained TS2339 (no render-sibling reach-ins). Split two ways: (a) 5 were the node's OWN namespace attaches `global.GLUDSP = …` / `global.GlucoDex = global.GlucoDex || …` (IIFE root `global` typed Window). These are DECLARED in a new node-scoped glucodex-globals.d.ts (`declare var GLUDSP/GlucoDex: any`), NOT inline-cast — CRUCIAL: a family of source-text safety gates in tests/dex-tests.js slices each node's `<node>LoadOwnExport` body on the VERBATIM attach marker (`gsrc.indexOf('global.GlucoDex = global.GlucoDex')` @ dex-tests.js:8464; ecgdex/cpapdex/ppgdex carry the same marker gate), so an inline `/** @type {any} */(global).X` cast rewrites that line, the marker vanishes, and the gate collapses (learned the hard way here). Declaring keeps the attach byte-identical. (b) the other 2 are genuine internal type-inference casts — `let run=/** @type {any} */(null)` and `/** @type {any} */(r).events=` — comment-only. So only (b) edits source → EXPORT-INERT re-bundle: GlucoDex manifestHash f136f2a11b74→e5a5e992c81c (+ Data Unifier/OverDex co-load re-bundle); GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. RULE for the rest: declare a DSP's own `global.<Node>` namespace attaches ambiently (byte-stable for the marker gates); only cast genuine internal property/inference errors. ADDED 2026-07-13 (6th pass): ecgdex-dsp.js — 16 errors, cleanly split: (a) 13 namespace/sibling globals declared in a new ecgdex-globals.d.ts — ECGDSP + ECGDex (own attaches, byte-stable for the ecgLoadOwnExport marker gate @ dex-tests.js:8581) + ECGMorph (a CONSUMED co-loaded sibling, ecgdex-morph.js, reached via a guarded `if(global.ECGMorph)`); (b) 2 genuine internal casts — a `/** @type {[string,number,number,string][]} */` tuple annotation on the walk-cadence `zoneDef` literal (fixes the TS2365 `>=`/`<` on `string|number` at :1643) and `/** @type {any} */(out)._relBase=` (a flag set on an accel array). Only (b) edits source → EXPORT-INERT: ECGDex manifestHash 84352a1f26b5→51d850b9f6b3 (+ Data Unifier/OverDex co-load); GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. ADDED 2026-07-13 (7th pass): cpapdex-dsp.js — 16 errors. (a) 5 cpap realm globals declared in a new cpapdex-globals.d.ts — CpapDsp/CPAPDex (own `root.X` attaches) + CpapEdf/CPAPCross/CpapFusion (consumed co-loaded siblings, guarded `root && root.X`). (b) Node builtins require+process (7 TS2591): declared `any` (checkJs has no @types/node) — BUT the two `require('./sibling.js')` CALL sites also needed `(/** @type {any} */(require))('./x')` source casts, because a bare declared `require` makes tsc RESOLVE+check the sibling module → cascading TS2306 through untyped IIFE globals (the cast breaks require-call recognition, bare `require` stays for Node; process needs no such cast). process declared, not cast (event-coupling's 1-off globalThis-cast idiom doesn't scale to cpapdex's many refs; promote require/process to a shared node-builtins.d.ts when a 2nd module needs them). (c) 1 genuine cast: `/** @type {any} */(a.t0==null) - /** @type {any} */(b.t0==null)` (TS2362/2363 — boolean subtraction in a sort comparator). Source edits (3, all comment-only) → EXPORT-INERT: CPAPDex manifestHash 86e8b88d185e→da98027c89b5 (orchestrators do NOT co-load cpapdex-dsp — no orchestrator re-bundle); GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. LESSON: a declared global `require` triggers tsc module resolution of its string-literal calls — always cast require CALL sites. ADDED 2026-07-13 (8th pass): ppgdex-dsp.js — 22 errors, but only ONE source edit. Declared in a new ppgdex-globals.d.ts: PPGDSP/PpgDex (own attaches, byte-stable for the ppgLoadOwnExport marker gate @ dex-tests.js:8877) + PPGMorph (consumed sibling ppgdex-morph.js) + BigInt (the ES2020 global for Polar ns-stamp parsing — this gate's lib is ES2017; declared `any` localized rather than bumping the fleet-wide lib). The lone source cast: `const td=/** @type {any} */(timeDomain(nn,cleanMask)||{})` — the `||{}` fallback made tsc infer `{}`, poisoning all 11 `td.<hrv-field>` accesses (one cast clears them). Comment-only → EXPORT-INERT: PpgDex manifestHash d4b2e772bc9c→b864cb285df8 (+ Data Unifier/OverDex co-load); GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. ADDED 2026-07-13 (9th pass): integrator-dsp.js — measured 17 (not the older 24; shared decls DexKernel/DexUnits/parseTimestamp had already resolved the rest). Declared in a new integrator-globals.d.ts: IntegratorDSP (own attach) + IntegratorTCH (optional temporal-consensus sibling) + GangliorProvenance (optional provenance sibling) — the last two reached via `typeof X!=='undefined'`/`window.X` guards. 6 comment-only source casts: `_ev`/`_pe` cast to any AT THEIR DECLARATIONS (`var _pe=/** @type {any} */(events[_pi])`) — NOT at the `.clampFloor`/`.sqiFloor` assignment, because a source-text gate asserts the verbatim `_pe.sqiFloor = true` write via a regex (dex-tests.js:2698) and casting the property access to `(_pe).sqiFloor` breaks that regex (learned the hard way — same class as the glucodex marker gate). `best` in the surge matcher (null→any, TS2538 index), `best.pos` in posAt (the `best &&` narrows the truthy branch to `never`), and the `interStart/interEnd` + `endMs-startMs` possibly-null arithmetic (TS18047). Export-inert: Integrator manifestHash ace24a7aa8fd→e73a6c528dd0 (+ OverDex co-load; NOT Data Unifier). GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. LESSON (reinforced): when casting to satisfy tsc near a fixed-shape object whose property WRITE is source-text-gated, cast the VARIABLE at declaration, not the property access. ADDED 2026-07-13 (10th pass — LAST DSP): oxydex-dsp.js — measured 68 (down from 90). Declared in a new oxydex-globals.d.ts: 9 co-loaded realm siblings (UP/renderAll ← oxydex-render, showError ← oxydex-app, upVO2category ← oxydex-profile, safeStyle/safeSet/safeEl/escHTML/computeCeilingBaselineArr ← oxydex-util) + 2 window state globals (_csvParseErrors ×30, _oxyReview) — declaring cleared 62 of 68 with zero source edit (the `root.OxyDex = OxyDex` attach does NOT error — root isn't Window-typed there). 4 comment-only source casts for the genuine internal errors: `_buf = /** @type {any} */(e.target.result)` (fixes the FileReader-result TS2769 `new Uint8Array` overload + TS2345 TextDecoder arg at once), and `ev.onset`/`e.artifact` on the fixed-shape desat-event objects (added-property writes/reads). ⚠ UNLIKE the other DSPs, oxydex-dsp.js is NOT in biome.json's formatter-override list (it is already biome-reflowed), so a touched file is FORMAT-checked by CI: my casts had to be run through `biome format --write` (it idiomatizes `/** @type {any} */ (x)` with a space) — the file is biome-ci-clean. Export-inert: OxyDex manifestHash 0b9099a1189d→5453679d7a50 (+ Data Unifier/OverDex co-load); GATE A/B green, no fixture output moved. ✅ TYPE-GATE COMPLETE — all 8 node DSPs (oxy/hrv/pulse/gluco/ecg/cpap/ppg via their own passes) + signal-orchestrate + the core adapter/spine layer are now under checkJs. Also fixed this pass: the 9th-pass //d2 text carried a regex with `\.`/`\s` (invalid JSON escapes — tsc's lenient JSONC parser tolerated it but strict JSON.parse choked); reworded to keep tsconfig.json strict-JSON-valid.
